title: Melicope fatuhivensis
intro: Melicope fatuhivensis, also known as the Fatu Hiva melicope, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the Marquesas Islands in French Polynesia. The plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial for the immune system and overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Melicope fatuhivensis include limonoids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Melicope fatuhivensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a type of citrus relative and is native to the island of Fatu Hiva in French Polynesia. The classification of Melicope fatuhivensis includes its kingdom as Plantae, its phylum as Magnoliophyta, and its order as Sapindales. This species is further classified into the genus Melicope and the family Rutaceae. Melicope fatuhivensis is also known as the Fatu Hiva melicope.
